page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, database operations, and web serving throughput remain in the average tier. However, a notable hardware tradeoff exists in memory performance; while cached small-block reads are average, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the weak and poor categories respectively, indicating potential bottlenecks for memory-intensive datasets.

Given the high compute-to-memory ratio and strong single-threaded performance, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference, but less optimal for memory-bound database systems or large-scale data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It delivers strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, though multi-core benchmarks and web serving throughput align with average tiers. A key hardware tradeoff is its memory subsystem, where uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak to poor. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is best suited for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 16 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM. It features strong single-core performance but exhibits weak uncached memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring strong single-core performance and dedicated AMD EPYC processors for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads.

Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Weak uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and light inference
